description_long: This is a traditional wooden yacht with a narrow, elongated hull and two prominent tanbark sails, reminiscent of a Chinese junk. The hull is constructed from wood, featuring a natural brown finish with visible planking and a slightly raised bow and stern. The sails are large, vertically ribbed, and cream-colored, attached to two masts positioned fore and aft. The deck appears open, with minimal superstructure, and there are no visible railings or seating areas. The yacht sits low in the water, suggesting a shallow draft suitable for calm or coastal waters. The overall design is classic and functional, emphasizing sailing performance and traditional aesthetics.
description: A classic wooden yacht with two large cream-colored sails and an open deck, featuring a narrow, elongated hull and traditional design elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow bow, central mast, and the forward edge of the sails.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the hull, both sails, and the open deck layout.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the stern, the aft mast, and the trailing edges of the sails.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the hull profile, both sails, and the open deck from the other side.
attribute_hull_design: Monohull with a narrow, elongated shape, slightly raised bow and stern, constructed from wood with visible planking and a natural brown finish.
attribute_deck_layout: Single open deck with no visible superstructure, railings, or seating; two masts are positioned fore and aft, and the deck appears uncluttered.
attribute_cabin_structure: No visible cabin structure; the deck is open with no raised cabin, windows, or doors apparent in any view.
